mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises economy-wide borrowing costs, which mechanically reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not negative on average during elevated FEDFUNDS regimes (t-test p > 0.05), or the Spearman correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and compress consumption expenditures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not negative on average when UNRATE exceeds 5.0% (t-test p > 0.05), or the frequency of positive returns in triggered instances exceeds 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts reveal a broad contraction in consumer and residential demand that feeds into lower corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or an interaction term between the two series adds no incremental explanatory power over main effects in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle demonstrates supply-side resilience and operating leverage that supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during FEDFUNDS > 4.5% regimes fail to produce positive average 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential construction activity and downstream demand for building materials and durable goods, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1400 does not produce negative average 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns in triggered instances is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in nonfarm payrolls reflect persistent labor market expansion that supports aggregate wage income and consumption expenditures, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS increases do not produce positive average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the Spearman correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of an elevated federal funds rate above 4 percent alongside a rising unemployment rate signals overtightening monetary policy that crushes aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and rising UNRATE does not produce negative average 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth predicts positive equity returns during post-shock recovery periods when output gap closure is the dominant macroeconomic theme and earnings revisions are strongly positive.
failure_condition: Accelerating GDPC1 during post-shock recovery periods does not produce positive average 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the information coefficient is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
